response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with genuine strengths in testing culture, mentoring, and some high-throughput systems work, but the profile does not meet the role's hard minimums. C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project, far short of the required 8 years, and the low-latency distributed-systems work was primarily in DLang for ad-tech rather than building or operating a cloud compute platform. There is no formal technical-leadership tenure over cloud or DevOps teams, and the recent career trajectory (TypeScript/Node product work, pricing systems, AI tooling) points away from the C++ infrastructure focus this role demands.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput microservices experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering-practice credentials: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus authored test frameworks and ran training workshops
Long, self-directed track record (19+ years) including founding and operating an open-source geo-data platform on Kubernetes
concerns: C++ experience is under one year, versus the 8-year minimum qualification
No production cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure ownership; systems work was in DLang and adjacent domains
No sustained formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers as required

key_factors.factor: Limited cloud platform / large-scale infra ownership
